relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus enters Capernaum after speaking to the people.
A valued servant nears death.
The centurion asks through elders for healing.
They appeal on the basis of worthiness.
They point to his love for the nation and synagogue.
The centurion sends humility before Jesus arrives.
He trusts the word without needing presence under his roof.
Authority is understood through command and obedience.
Jesus marvels at trust found outside expected boundaries.
The servants return to health already present.
Jesus comes to Nain with disciples and crowd.
A widow’s only son is carried out through the gate.
Compassion sees the mother before the miracle.
Touch stops the funeral procession and calls the young man to rise.
Life returns and the son is given back to his mother.
The people name visitation and prophet-presence.
The word spreads through Judea and beyond.
John hears reports through his disciples.
John asks whether Jesus is the coming one.
The question is carried directly to Jesus.
In that hour, healing and sight answer the question.
Jesus sends back Isaiah-shaped evidence: sight, walking, cleansing, hearing, rising, good news to poor.
Blessed is the one not scandalized by Jesus’ way.
Jesus asks what they went to see in the wilderness.
John was not palace softness.
John is prophet and more than prophet.
He is the messenger preparing the way.
John is great, yet the reign opens a new order.
People and tax collectors justify the Divine through John’s baptism.
The Pharisees and lawyers reject the counsel by refusing the baptism.
The generation is compared to children unwilling to respond.
They refuse both dance and lament.
John’s austerity is dismissed as spirit-trouble.
Jesus’ table-fellowship is dismissed as excess.
Wisdom is justified by her children.
A Pharisee invites Jesus to table.
A misaligned woman enters with alabaster ointment.
Her tears, hair, kisses, and ointment become embodied love.
The host judges touch through reputation.
Jesus opens a teaching for Simon.
Two debtors hold two measures of release.
Forgiveness awakens love.
Simon sees the logic of greater release and greater love.
Jesus turns him toward seeing the woman.
Her kisses reveal hospitality the host withheld.
Her anointing surpasses expected honor.
Much release becomes much love.
Jesus speaks release directly to her.
The table questions who can release misalignment.
Her trust has saved/healed her; she goes into peace.
